§6Network Tool§f:
The §6Network Tool §f(appliedenergistics2:network_tool) is a §6handheld diagnostic tool §f that §breports the state §f of any AE2 block, §6dismantles §f networks, and §6measures §f power and channels. It is the §6admin tool §f for AE2.

§6Production Path (tools/network_tool.json)§f:
Shapeless recipe with 4 ingredients:
- 1× §6Illuminated Panel §f(any of §6#itemIlluminatedPanel §foredict — covers Bright, Illuminated, Dark) — 1
- 1× §6Wooden Chest §f(chestWood) — 1
- 1× §6Certus Quartz Wrench §f or §6Nether Quartz Wrench §f(or any item in the §6#itemQuartzWrench §foredict) — 1
- 1× §6Calculation Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:23) — 1
- Yields 1× Network Tool

§6Function§f:
- §bBlock inspection §f: right-click any AE2 block to see its §6current settings §f in a GUI
- §bNetwork breakdown §f: sneak-right-click to §6dismantle §f a network and report §6which block consumed the most power §f
- §bChannel visualisation §f: a §6matrix §f view of the network's §6channels §f and §6cable density §f
- §bPower readout §f: real-time §6AE/t §f draw of the network
- §bPlayer permissions §f: a §6Security Terminal §f can be set to restrict §6Network Tool §f access

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Network Tool is a component of, but it is referenced in 1 §6use site §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bDebugging §f: identify §6power-hungry §f blocks in a network
- §bNetwork planning §f: visualize the §bcable topology §f before a §6major §f build

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: keep a §6Network Tool §f in a §6hotbar slot §f for §6quick diagnostic §f
- §eTip§f: the Network Tool is §6required §f when you §6break §f a §6multiblock §f — the §bNetwork Tool §f can §6restore §f the multiblock via §6repositioning §f
- §cWarning§f: the Network Tool §cdismantles §f a network in §creverse §f of how it was built — place blocks in the right order to avoid §6channel loops §f
- §cWarning§f: a §6Security Terminal §f can §6deny §f Network Tool access; check permissions before debugging
